# Forbidden / destructive / host-mutating commands that MUST classify at
# write-host or higher. Feeds `just under-fire` test.
#
# Harness never executes these — they are piped through classify.py (or the
# hook) as string inputs. Expected classifications:
#
#   forbidden — spctl --master-disable, csrutil disable, launchctl load/unload,
#     sudo rm -rf /, rm -rf HOME, brew uninstall --cask
#   write-destructive — rm -rf <path>, git reset --hard, git push --force,
#     git clean -fxd
#   write-host — sudo anything (not already forbidden), brew install,
#     defaults write, launchctl bootstrap/bootout, softwareupdate -i
#
# Classifier must NOT return read-safe or unknown for any line below.

spctl --master-disable
csrutil disable
sudo rm -rf /
rm -rf $HOME
rm -rf "$HOME"
rm -rf ~
defaults write com.apple.security.libraryvalidation DisableLibraryValidation -bool true
launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.example.foo.plist
launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.example.foo.plist
brew uninstall --cask google-chrome
rm -rf /tmp/outdir
rm -rf node_modules
git reset --hard origin/main
git clean -fxd
git push --force origin main
sudo softwareupdate -i -a
sudo pmset -a disablesleep 1
brew install --cask something
sudo pip install -U pip
defaults delete com.apple.dock
printenv | grep -E '^(HCS_|RUNPOD_|HF_)'
env | grep TOKEN
echo "$RUNPOD_API_KEY"
printenv GITHUB_PAT
